page: Amazon Web Services i7i.metal-48xl is a storage-optimized bare-metal server powered by Intel Xeon 8559C processors. It features 192 vCPUs across 96 physical cores with a base clock speed of 2.4 GHz and a 320 MB L3 cache. The architecture supports advanced instruction sets including Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) and AVX-512, which accelerate matrix multiplication and cryptographic operations.

The server is equipped with 1536.0 GB of high-speed DDR5 memory operating at 6 GHz, providing 8.0 GB of RAM per physical core. For local storage, it bundles 45,000 GB of NVMe SSD capacity, paired with a baseline network bandwidth of 100 Gbps. This hardware profile delivers top-tier multi-core performance, as evidenced by top 10% rankings in stress-ng multi-core CPU benchmarks, PassMark CPU tests, and multi-threaded bzip3 decompression.

Benchmark data highlights exceptional memory read bandwidth of 13,262.17 GB/sec for cached blocks and 576.98 GB/sec for uncached large blocks. This throughput supports intensive database operations, with Redis SET performance reaching 223.07 million operations per second. While multi-core and memory bandwidth metrics reside in the top tier, single-core performance is more moderate, with stress-ng single-core CPU and PassMark memory scores falling into the average category.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the i7i.metal-48xl offers high resource density by consolidating massive NVMe storage, high-speed DDR5 RAM, and 192 dedicated vCPUs onto a single bare-metal instance. This configuration is highly optimized for large-scale relational databases, NoSQL platforms, high-throughput static web serving, and local LLM inference workloads.
description: Amazon Web Services i7i.metal-48xl is a storage-optimized bare-metal server designed for data-intensive workloads. It features 192 vCPUs from Intel Xeon 8559C processors, 1536.0 GB of DDR5 memory running at 6 GHz, and 45,000 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. The system delivers top-tier multi-core and database performance, supported by 100 Gbps baseline network bandwidth and advanced CPU instructions like Intel AMX and AVX-512. While single-core performance and memory latency benchmarks sit in the average tier, its massive parallel processing capabilities and high-speed memory bandwidth excel in data-heavy environments. This bare-metal instance is highly suited for large-scale databases, Redis deployments, and high-throughput data processing pipelines.
og_description: Amazon Web Services i7i.metal-48xl is a bare-metal server featuring 192 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 1536 GB DDR5 RAM, and 45,000 GB NVMe SSD storage, delivering top-tier database and multi-core performance.

tagline: A high-density bare-metal server combining massive NVMe storage, DDR5 memory, and 192 dedicated vCPUs for demanding database workloads.
bullet_points: 192 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs with AVX-512 and AMX instruction sets
1536.0 GB of high-speed 6 GHz DDR5 memory
45,000 GB of high-performance local NVMe SSD storage
100 Gbps baseline network bandwidth for high-throughput data transfer
Top-tier multi-core, database, and Redis SET performance
Optimized for large-scale databases, NoSQL, and data-intensive pipelines
